issue_title: Formalize API for Computation object.
issue_body:
### What is wrong?

Currently, the `Computation` object houses *lots* of information and sub-APIs for things like maniputlating the stack, consuming gas, etc.  This creates a lot of public API surface area since we are effectively support *two* public APIs for anything needed from a sub-property of the `Computation` object.

- `Computation.memory` is the API for getting at the `Memory`
- `Computation.memory.read` is the API for reading from memory.

### How can it be fixed

I propose moving **all** sub-APIs to private properties and only exposing them through a single public API on the `Computation` object itself.

For example.

- `Computation.memory -> Computation._memory`
- `Computation.memory.read() -> Computation.memory_read()`

In the process we should organize the body of the `Computation` object by subject/sub-API meaning that we co-locate all of the *memory* APIs together, and so on for each category.

#### Maybe use abbreviated suffixes

Some of these accessors can be quite long:

- `Computation.gas_meter.consume_gas`

Maybe it's ok for us to shorten these using a standard abbreviated prefix.  This trades one type of readability for another and since these are reasonably *low level* APIs that are only consumed from within EVM execution, I think that I'm comfortable with this trade-off.

- `Computation.gm_consume_gas()`

#### Maybe create a common base class

It might also be good to create a `BaseComputation` class which contains stubs for all of the public APIs which raise `NotImplementedError` exceptions.  This serves as a sort of documentation from within the code for the API for the computation object.

A possible argument against this is that the `sharding` changes may introduce new APIs which are not used pre-sharding, and in some cases, potentially removing APIs which are no longer part of the sharding EVM.

issue_title: Don't encourage using ConciseContract or ImplicitContract as `ContractFactoryClass`
issue_body:
### What was wrong?

Documentation encourages using `ConciseContract` and `ImplicitContract` as a `ContractFactoryClass`. They are not proper subclasses of `Contract`, which causes confusion when the API changes (like `deploy()` is no longer available)

### How can it be fixed?

1. Remove references to using these classes as a factory in the documentation
2. Show preferred implementation instead
3. Show using methods on the core contract before wrapping up in these convenience classes
2. Add a deprecation warning when using `ConciseContract` or `ImplicitContract` as a factory.

Preferred implementation of `ConciseContract`, with example core contract methods:
```py
token_contract = web3.eth.contract(abi=...)
token = ConciseContract(token_contract)

token_contract.deploy(...) 
# wait for deployment transaction to be mined

 # return my balance
token.balanceOf(MY_ADDR)

 # returns my balance with the core contract (exactly equivalent to previous line)
token_contract.functions.balanceOf(MY_ADDR).call()
```

issue_title: Conda develop build_ext inplace on windows incorrectly uses root environment
issue_body:
On windows, when using conda develop with the -b build_ext inplace flag, the build process incorrectly uses the root environment.

Take the following  setup.py

```
import sys
print sys.executable
import numpy
```

With the following environment:

```
[conda-dev-bug] C:\Projects\conda-dev-bug>conda list
# packages in environment at C:\Miniconda2\envs\conda-dev-bug:
#
mkl                       11.3.1                        0
numpy                     1.11.0                   py27_0
pip                       8.1.1                    py27_1
python                    2.7.11                        4
setuptools                20.7.0                   py27_0
vs2008_runtime            9.00.30729.1                  0
wheel                     0.29.0                   py27_0
```

When I run the in place build, the root environment is used, and numpy is not found.

```
[conda-dev-bug] C:\Projects\conda-dev-bug>conda develop -b .
C:\Miniconda2\python.exe
Traceback (most recent call last):
  File "C:\Projects\conda-dev-bug\setup.py", line 4, in <module>
    import numpy
ImportError: No module named numpy
Command failed: python C:\Projects\conda-dev-bug\setup.py build_ext --inplace
```


On Linux the same set up works without any issues:

```
(conda-dev-bug)[marknuttall@abcdef qt]$ conda develop -b .
/home/marknuttall/miniconda2/envs/conda-dev-bug/bin/python
Completed: python /home/marknuttall/qt/setup.py build_ext --inplace
===============================================
added /home/marknuttall/qt
completed operation for: /home/marknuttall/qt
```

As a workaround people are simply using the root environment when developing, but of course this removes a lot of the benefits of conda. Any other ideas very gratefully received.

issue_title: pre/post script failures appear to be fatal but only produce WARNING
issue_body:
While looking into #5417 I have discovered that the `pre-unlink.sh` script's failure is actually causing the entire update to bork out, but I am not given any indication that this has happened.

Here is the fragment showing the `WARNING` followed by (effective) failure:

```
Proceed ([y]/n)? y

pysocks-1.6.6- 100% |#############################################################| Time: 0:00:00   1.73 MB/s
urllib3-1.21.1 100% |#############################################################| Time: 0:00:00   4.76 MB/s
widgetsnbexten 100% |#############################################################| Time: 0:00:00   3.63 MB/s
WARNING conda.core.link:run_script(519): pre-unlink script failed for package conda-forge::widgetsnbextension-2.0.0-py36_0
consider notifying the package maintainer
(root) twitter $
```

And also strangely it appears that either the unlink phase and package downloads are concurrent or unlinks are done one-at-a-time with each download, because on my first invocation of the update command 3 packages were downloaded before the pre-unlink script failure caused the process to abort, but when I immediately re-issued it more downloads occurred and the command completed successfully.  I would have expected all the downloads to complete before conda started running `pre-unlink.sh` scripts.  If that is a reasonable expectation then perhaps there is another issue that should also be reported.

Attached is the trace of executing exactly the same command (`conda update -n spark --all`) 3 times in succession:

[warning_trace.txt](https://github.com/conda/conda/files/1159906/warning_trace.txt)

First time: downloads 3 packages and then fails on `pre-unlink.sh` with a WARNING.  Unclear that the update failed.

Second time: many more packages downloaded, appears to complete successfully.

Third time: confirms that the second invocation worked: no new packages to install/update.

issue_title: Activate issue with parallel activations - "File exists" in symlinked path
issue_body:
I'm working in a cluster environment where I am trying to activate a conda environment in multiple parallel processes. After recently upgrading to the latest release (4.1.2), I've been receiving a "File exists" error in some of those processes, similar to the below:

```
Traceback (most recent call last):
  File "/symln_home/anaconda/bin/conda", line 6, in <module>
    sys.exit(main())
  File "/Users/tomflem/anaconda/lib/python2.7/site-packages/conda/cli/main.py", line 48, in main
    activate.main()
  File "/Users/tomflem/anaconda/lib/python2.7/site-packages/conda/cli/activate.py", line 163, in main
    conda.install.symlink_conda(prefix, root_dir, shell)

    symlink_fn(root_file, prefix_file)

```

I've been able to repro this issue on a laptop when my PATH to the root anaconda install contains a symlinked directory ("symln_home," in this example). Doesn't seem to be a problem when the PATH is symlink-free. The below has produced the error consistently for me on OS X:

``` python
from multiprocessing import Pool
import os

call = ['source activate myenv && echo $PATH' for i in range(30)]

```

Is this a bug, or are there any workarounds for this particular use case? In my cluster environment, my HOME directory (where I've installed anaconda) is symlinked, which I would assume is a fairly common setup in shared computing (I don't admin it, so unfortunately I can't change that aspect).

issue_title: Issue in AbstractDxlIO._open() when using protocol 2, sync_read and not USB2AX
issue_body:
The code starting at line 133 in abstract_io.py tries to broadcast a ping. If there are Dynamixles that use protocol 2 (ex. XL-320) and they are interfaced using a different interface than USB2AX (ex. USB2Dynamixel) the code will fail after trying 500 times. This is due to the fact that (it's not very clear in the manual) with protocol 2 broadcast ping does not return a status packet. See this [link](http://support.robotis.com/en/product/actuator/dynamixel_pro/communication/instruction_status_packet.htm) - and scroll to the Ping instruction, point 3.
As a result creating an Dxl320IO with sync_read will issue an error:

`from pypot import dynamixel`
`dxl = dynamixel.Dxl320IO('/dev/tty.usbserial-AL01QHDB', 1000000, use_sync_read=True)`
`Traceback (most recent call last):`
`  File "<stdin>", line 1, in <module>`
`  File "/Users/alexandrusonea/anaconda/lib/python2.7/site-packages/pypot/dynamixel/io/abstract_io.py", line 76, in __init__`
`    self.open(port, baudrate, timeout)`
`  File "/Users/alexandrusonea/anaconda/lib/python2.7/site-packages/pypot/dynamixel/io/abstract_io.py", line 99, in open`
`    self._open(port, baudrate, timeout)`
`  File "/Users/alexandrusonea/anaconda/lib/python2.7/site-packages/pypot/dynamixel/io/abstract_io.py", line 147, in _open`
`    raise DxlError('could not connect to the port {}'.format(self.port))`
`pypot.dynamixel.io.abstract_io.DxlError: could not connect to the port /dev/tty.usbserial-AL01QHDB`

issue_title: resize throws "Python int too large to convert to C long"
issue_body:
Calling `mycarray.resize(NEW_SIZE)` sometime throws quite a strange error:
```
  File "bcolz/carray_ext.pyx", line 1560, in bcolz.carray_ext.carray.resize (bcolz\carray_ext.c:20202)
  File "bcolz/carray_ext.pyx", line 1403, in bcolz.carray_ext.carray.append (bcolz\carray_ext.c:18995)
OverflowError: Python int too large to convert to C long
```

Consider, for example, the following code:
```
import numpy as np
import bcolz

INI_SIZE = 1e7
NEW_SIZE = 27*1e7

#n = np.arange(INI_SIZE)
n = np.ones(INI_SIZE)
#n = np.zeros(INI_SIZE)

a = bcolz.carray(n)
print repr(a)

a.resize(NEW_SIZE)
print repr(a)
```

With NEW_SIZE is 300 million the program falls with OverflowError.
However with NEW_SIZE = 270 million it finishes without errors, but something is really wrong here:
```
carray((10000000,), float64)
  nbytes: 76.29 MB; cbytes: 668.16 KB; ratio: 116.93
  cparams := cparams(clevel=5, shuffle=True, cname='blosclz')
[ 1.  1.  1. ...,  1.  1.  1.]
carray((-266870912,), float64)
  nbytes: -2134967296; cbytes: 1.41 MB; ratio: -1444.05
  cparams := cparams(clevel=5, shuffle=True, cname='blosclz')
[ 0.]
```
After resizing the array gets a negative size.

In your installation the error causing NEW_SIZE might differ, but you can easily find numbers which cause OverflowError and a negative size issue.

issue_title: Improve speed of pileup engine
issue_body:
pysam's approach is to provide a lazy wrapper around htslib constructs. This approach is violated
in IteratorColumn. IteratorColumn instantiates a full list of AlignedSegment objects even if none or only part of these are used.

A proposed solution is to return a Proxy object in makePileupColumn that will provide lazy access to the aligned reads in a pileup column.

issue_title: DeleteSnapshot API timing out, failing immediately on retry
issue_body:
I'm trying to delete snapshots on a recurring schedule via the Python API on AWS Lambda. I'm hitting long wait times for the deletion to complete. It eventually will fail with a 504 gateway timeout but then hits a 404 on retry because the snapshot no longer exists.

Example sequence for a single snapshot:
```
2017-04-25T00:59:51.214Z Deleting snapshot logs-20170424221413...
2017-04-25T01:00:51.85Z DELETE http://mysescluster:9200/_snapshot/some_snapshots/logs-20170424221413 [status:504 request:59.871s]
2017-04-25T01:00:51.258Z DELETE http://myescluster:9200/_snapshot/some_snapshots/logs-20170424221413 [status:404 request:0.173s]
```

The 1 minute timeout is coming from my Elasticsearch cluster being behind an AWS ELB with a 1 minute idle timeout. I can bump that and the Elasticsearch connection timeout to give it more room.

What's confusing though is that the Snapshot seems to delete since the retry hits a 404. So it looks like the delete succeeds but doesn't finish the request. 

Is there any way to avoid waiting for completion when calling `DeleteSnapshot` via API? It doesn't look like this method supports it in the underlying ES transport method.

Elasticsearch version: 5.2.2
Curator version: 5.0.1
Elasticsearch-py version: 5.1.0

issue_title: Indicate `https` under `scheme` in `repr(client)`
issue_body:
If you make a client: `client = es.Elasticsearch(my_https_url)`, and you type `print repr(client)`, you will see confusing information.

You will see `u'use_ssl': True`, but you will also see `u'scheme': u'http'`.  This is a little bit worrisome because, if we were actually using ssl, you might expect to see `https`.

I believe the behavior comes from [this line](https://github.com/elastic/elasticsearch-py/blob/05ad5ef96bf958a1482274c57feb56a8d873dc8d/elasticsearch/client/__init__.py#L47).

I'd prefer to update it, so that `'use_ssl': True` means you also see `'scheme': 'https'`.  Then again, `https` is just a secure version of `http` so I guess the information displayed isn't technically _wrong_

issue_title: Azure Stack inconsistency
issue_body:
I am using this module indirectly via Ansible however I am unable to create storage accounts.

The error I receive is:

```
An exception occurred during task execution. To see the full traceback, use -vvv. The error was: msrest.exceptions.SerializationError: Unable to build a model: <SkuName.standard_lrs: 'Standard_LRS'> is not valid value for enum <enum 'SkuName'>, DeserializationError: <SkuName.standard_lrs: 'Standard_LRS'> is not valid value for enum <enum 'SkuName'>
fatal: [localhost]: FAILED! => {"changed": false, "module_stderr": "Traceback (most recent call last):
  File "/tmp/ansible_94zbHG/ansible_module_azure_rm_storageaccount.py", line 462, in <module>
    main()
  File "/tmp/ansible_94zbHG/ansible_module_azure_rm_storageaccount.py", line 459, in main
    AzureRMStorageAccount()
  File "/tmp/ansible_94zbHG/ansible_module_azure_rm_storageaccount.py", line 204, in __init__
    supports_check_mode=True)
  File "/tmp/ansible_94zbHG/ansible_modlib.zip/ansible/module_utils/azure_rm_common.py", line 287, in __init__
  File "/tmp/ansible_94zbHG/ansible_module_azure_rm_storageaccount.py", line 240, in exec_module
    self.results['state'] = self.create_account()
  File "/tmp/ansible_94zbHG/ansible_module_azure_rm_storageaccount.py", line 416, in create_account
    poller = self.storage_client.storage_accounts.create(self.resource_group, self.name, parameters)
  File "/usr/lib/python2.7/site-packages/azure/mgmt/storage/v2016_01_01/operations/storage_accounts_operations.py", line 164, in create
    body_content = self._serialize.body(parameters, 'StorageAccountCreateParameters')
  File "/usr/lib/python2.7/site-packages/msrest/serialization.py", line 452, in body
    SerializationError, "Unable to build a model: "+str(err), err)
  File "/usr/lib/python2.7/site-packages/msrest/exceptions.py", line 48, in raise_with_traceback
    raise error
msrest.exceptions.SerializationError: Unable to build a model: <SkuName.standard_lrs: 'Standard_LRS'> is not valid value for enum <enum 'SkuName'>, DeserializationError: <SkuName.standard_lrs: 'Standard_LRS'> is not valid value for enum <enum 'SkuName'>
", "module_stdout": "", "msg": "MODULE FAILURE", "rc": 0}```

This is because the storage storage type is in fact StandardLRS (no "_"). Change the value to reflect this this results in:

```
fatal: [localhost]: FAILED! => {"changed": false, "msg": "value of account_type must be one of: Premium_LRS,Standard_GRS,Standard_LRS,Standard_RAGRS,Standard_ZRS, got: StandardLRS"}
```

as there is field validation. I suspect this is one of many issue that will crop up of this nature but since the Azure Stack is meant ot be an extension of Azure consistency where features are supported is essential. Is this team working with the Azure Stack team to validate it works correctly?

issue_title: Azure python SDK file descriptor leak under multi-thread stress enviroment
issue_body:
We recently found file descriptor leaking while stress testing with Azure solution. Two APIs we nailed down so far:
1.	subnets.list
2.	storage_account.list_key
We are using Azure 2.0.0 running on ubuntu 14.0.4. Our application use default number of file descriptor 1024(soft limit) and 4096 hard limit. 
We found file descriptor leaking is caused by using the python uuid lib under multi-thread environment.

issue_title: Version numbers, packaging, and releases
issue_body:
Hi,

We are trying to provide the azure-sdk-for-python as a package to openSUSE and SUSE Linux Enterprise users. We are also using this as a basis to develop azurectl. While this is possible today it has become much more cumbersome since the reorganization of the project structure a while back. Also there is some inconsistency that can cause confusion and it would be great if these things can be eliminated and addressed.

Let me start out by describing what we would like to see and what is common accepted practice. We would like to see releases show up on pypi as "some-name.tar.gz", this can be the name of the release tarball from the github project, i.e. azure-sdk-for-python-1.0.3.tar.gz or some other name. This tarball should contain all the parts that are considered part of the SDK (currently that's apparently  azure-mgmt, azure-servicebus, azure-servicemanagement-legacy, and azure-storage only) and preferably a setup.py that conforms to common accepted practices.

As it stands today pypi provides either the components azure-mgmt, azure-servicebus, azure-servicemanagement-legacy, and azure-storage as download or a "wrapper" azure-1.0.3.zip. The wrapper itself provides basically no source code. Since the components are versioned separately and supplied as separate downloads this causes a great deal of confusion about a.) the version number that the SDK should have and b.) what is part of the SDK. According to the github release the following are part of the release (1.0.3):

azure
azure-common
azure-mgmt
azure-mgmt-common
azure-mgmt-compute
azure-mgmt-network
azure-mgmt-nspkg
azure-mgmt-resource
azure-mgmt-storage
azure-nspkg
azure-servicebus
azure-servicemanagement-legacy

while according to pypi only

azure-mgmt
azure-servicebus
azure-servicemanagement-legacy
azure-storage

are part of the sdk release.

Can this be sorted out such that we can download 1 tarball from pypi that represents the source code that the primary developers of the code base consider to be a release?

Thanks in advance

issue_title: Blob objects returned by list_blobs has None metadata properties
issue_body:
azure-storage==0.32.0
Python 3.4.3
requests==2.10.0

If You use:

```
from azure.storage import CloudStorageAccount
ACC = CloudStorageAccount(account_name, account_key)
SRV = ACC.create_block_blob_service()
GEN = SRV.list_blobs(container_name)
for blob in GEN:
    print(blob.metadata)
```

to get list of blobs in container and their metadata, You will have:

None
None
None
etc...

But if You use:

```
blob0 = SRV.get_blob_properties(container_name, blob_name)
print(blob0)
```

Everything is OK:

{'val1': 'foo', 'val2': 'blah'}

In both cases we have azure.storage.blob.models.Blob object so there should be 'metadata' property as in Model.

issue_title: Get nbgrader via jupyterhub to respect directory structure?
issue_body:
### Operating system

Ubuntu 16

### `nbgrader --version`

nbgrader version 0.5.3

### `jupyterhub --version` (if used with JupyterHub)

0.7.2

### Notebook - Help, About version info

5.0.0

### Expected behavior

I carefully followed the directory structure plan. I am able to get jupyterhub to spawn notebooks for the instructor and the students. That works great. The problem is that nbgrader seems to want the `source` directory to be in the root of the filesystem for the users (e.g., `/home/instructor`), even though the class materials are in `/home/instructor/course100/source`. This is a problem for using the buttons in the Jupyter Notebook to generate and  distribute assignments because it seems as if those buttons are hard-wired to look for the root directories of the students and the  instructor (e.g.,`/home/student01`) instead of using (or creating) `/home/student01/course100/`. I can get around this problem by shelling into the instance and running `nbgrader` from the commandline but that's not a good solution in general for the jupyterhub  configuration I am trying to build.

### Actual behavior
`nbgrader` wants everything to be in the `$HOME` directories of the instructor and students.

### Steps to reproduce the behavior

Follow the wiki instructions to the letter and deploy with jupyterhub.

issue_title: nbgrader push and pull for assignment distribution and turning in
issue_body:
I think I have figured out a simple way of creating two new nbgrader commands, push and pull, that will make it easy for instructors and students to manage assignments. Initially it commands will assume that nbgrader is deployed on a single JupyterHub instance, but I think it won't be difficult to make the design scale to other usage cases. The big issue that this will solve is allowing multiple instructors teaching multiple classes with different students in each class. It doesn't require instructors to have any special permissions on the system so the sys-admin and dev-ops load will be simple.

The idea is the build a set of directories in a centralized place like `/srv/nbgrader/exchange.` I am still working out the details, but I think I see a way to create a read-only outbound area for instructors and a write-only inbound area for students to turn things in. By saving students turned in work with uuids in the filenames, we can prevent other students from overwriting each others work, and can also have a full auditable record of each time a student submits something (usually we will just grade the last one).

Here is the rough flow (assuming the usernames are instructor and student1, student2):

To distribute an assignment directory homework1 to students:

    nbgrader push homework1 instructor.phys202-2015.homework1

To grab this assignment, students would do:

    nbgrader pull instructor.phys202-2015.homework1

To turn their work in, students would do:

    nbgrader push homework1 instructor.phys202-2015.homework1

To get all students work, instructor would do:

    nbgrader pull instructor.phys202-2015.homework1

This extremely terse syntax assumes that nbgrader push/pull will be run as the instructor/student user. It also assumes that the unix usernames match the nbgrader usernames, but I think that could be relaxed easily. I will be building this today and submit a PR, but I have to have this working by tomorrow so I may just go ahead and merge the PR when it is in good shape and we can iterate in master.

issue_title: planemo lint XSD check unaware of legacy parameter attribute default_value
issue_body:
While `<param value="..." ...>` is prefered, Galaxy still supports `<param default_value="..." ...>` for a `ColumnListParameter`, as can be seen here:

https://github.com/galaxyproject/galaxy/blob/dev/lib/galaxy/tools/parameters/basic.py#L1005

``` python
        # Legacy style default value specification...
        self.default_value = input_source.get( "default_value", None )
        if self.default_value is None:
            # Newer style... more in line with other parameters.
            self.default_value = input_source.get( "value", None )
        if self.default_value is not None:
            self.default_value = ColumnListParameter._strip_c( self.default_value )
```

The XSD should be aware of this legacy attribute, which currently causes lint failures, e.g. https://travis-ci.org/peterjc/galaxy_blast/jobs/167595623

```
+Linting tool /home/travis/build/peterjc/galaxy_blast/tools/blastxml_to_top_descr/blastxml_to_top_descr.xml

/tmp/tmp6TctTh:32:0:ERROR:SCHEMASV:SCHEMAV_CVC_COMPLEX_TYPE_3_2_1: Element 'param', attribute 'default_value': The attribute 'default_value' is not allowed.
/tmp/tmp6TctTh:33:0:ERROR:SCHEMASV:SCHEMAV_CVC_COMPLEX_TYPE_3_2_1: Element 'param', attribute 'default_value': The attribute 'default_value' is not allowed.

issue_title: Unresolveable I202
issue_body:
Hi there,

I upgraded `flake8-import-order` for [flake8](https://gitlab.com/pycqa/flake) and it started failing on L22 of our `src/flake8/__init__.py` file. I couldn't figure out how to resolve it so I simply had to silence it. Below are the contents of the file:

```py
"""Top-level module for Flake8.

This module

- initializes logging for the command-line tool
- tracks the version of the package
- provides a way to configure logging for the command-line tool

.. autofunction:: flake8.configure_logging

"""
import logging
try:
    from logging import NullHandler
except ImportError:
    class NullHandler(logging.Handler):
        """Shim for version of Python < 2.7."""

        def emit(self, record):
            """Do nothing."""
            pass
import sys  # noqa: I202

LOG = logging.getLogger(__name__)
LOG.addHandler(NullHandler())

# Clean up after LOG config
del NullHandler

__version__ = '3.5.0'
__version_info__ = tuple(int(i) for i in __version__.split('.') if i.isdigit())


# There is nothing lower than logging.DEBUG (10) in the logging library,
# but we want an extra level to avoid being too verbose when using -vv.
_EXTRA_VERBOSE = 5
logging.addLevelName(_EXTRA_VERBOSE, 'VERBOSE')

_VERBOSITY_TO_LOG_LEVEL = {
    # output more than warnings but not debugging info
    1: logging.INFO,  # INFO is a numerical level of 20
    # output debugging information
    2: logging.DEBUG,  # DEBUG is a numerical level of 10
    # output extra verbose debugging information
    3: _EXTRA_VERBOSE,
}

LOG_FORMAT = ('%(name)-25s %(processName)-11s %(relativeCreated)6d '
              '%(levelname)-8s %(message)s')


def configure_logging(verbosity, filename=None, logformat=LOG_FORMAT):
    """Configure logging for flake8.

    :param int verbosity:
        How verbose to be in logging information.
    :param str filename:
        Name of the file to append log information to.
        If ``None`` this will log to ``sys.stderr``.
        If the name is "stdout" or "stderr" this will log to the appropriate
        stream.
    """
    if verbosity <= 0:
        return
    if verbosity > 3:
        verbosity = 3

    log_level = _VERBOSITY_TO_LOG_LEVEL[verbosity]

    if not filename or filename in ('stderr', 'stdout'):
        fileobj = getattr(sys, filename or 'stderr')
        handler_cls = logging.StreamHandler
    else:
        fileobj = filename
        handler_cls = logging.FileHandler

    handler = handler_cls(fileobj)
    handler.setFormatter(logging.Formatter(logformat))
    LOG.addHandler(handler)
    LOG.setLevel(log_level)
    LOG.debug('Added a %s logging handler to logger root at %s',
              filename, __name__)
```

issue_title: Reverter could be confused by system clock changes
issue_body: If the client is run with a system clock at time t1, and then run with an earlier time t0, `reverter.rollback_checkpoints` would wind up broken.  That would be especially sever in case of an installer error, but would also affect use of the `revert` verb.

issue_title: Add .txt extension to ACME challenge URL for automatic Content-Type
issue_body:
Currently when performing manual authentication, you ask the user to create a file with a certain file name, and then reconfigure their webserver to serve that with `Content-Type: text/plain`. For example `http://example.org/.well-known/acme-challenge/GUu94CoL-MRy4Lr4VpCLV4uG4kEqm4hDQ_9i8XMOixo`

You could make it a lot easier by appending `.txt` to the challenge URL (filename), so no reconfiguration needs to take place.

issue_title: Producer in child process not sending messages
issue_body:
I was looking into using python's multiprocessing library to handle producing messages from a child process. However, it seems that messages are not being sent when produced from a child process. My (simplified) setup:

```python
import multiprocessing

from confluent_kafka import Producer


class MyProducer(multiprocessing.Process):
    def __init__(self, kafka_bootstrap: str, kafka_topic: str, queue: multiprocessing.JoinableQueue):
        multiprocessing.Process.__init__(self)
        self.daemon = True
        self.kafka_topic = kafka_topic
        conf = {
            'bootstrap.servers': kafka_bootstrap,
            'api.version.request': True
        }
        self.producer = Producer(**conf)
        self.queue = queue

    def run(self):
        while True:
            msg = self.queue.get()
            if msg is None:
                print('Shutting down child process...')
                break
            self.producer.produce(self.kafka_topic, msg)
            print('flushing')
            self.producer.flush()  # Added in temporarily to show this issue
            print('flushed')  # Never prints
            self.queue.task_done()

if __name__ == '__main__':
    msg_queue = multiprocessing.JoinableQueue()
    child = MyProducer('localhost:9092', 'mytopic', msg_queue)
    child.start()

    messages = ['M1', 'M2', 'M3', None]
    for m in messages:
        msg_queue.put(m)

    msg_queue.join()
```

The above prints the output `flushing`, but never prints `flushed`.

If, instead of forking off a child process, I take the logic of `MyProducer.run()` and execute it in the main process instead, the script works as expected. Any insight into what's happening here?

issue_title: Parse failure for a date string with redundant timezone specification
issue_body:
I found some dates in email headers that look like:

```
Thu, 5 Aug 2010 10:38:28 -0400 (GMT-04:00)
Wed, 28 Jul 2010 10:38:18 -0400 (GMT-04:00)
Mon, 26 Jul 2010 10:38:20 -0400 (GMT-04:00)
```

`dateutil.parser.parse` raises `ValueError("Unknown string format")` on them.

All the examples I can find end like the above in `-0400 (GMT-04:00)`, so for them the part in parentheses is redundant. I don't know if that's safe to assume in general, though.

issue_title: Z3 Java API toString() method Bug?
Caching sat results?
issue_body:
So, in playing around with Z3 more, I've discovered that it does not appear to cache sat results. For instance, assuming I had a bunch of assertions in the solver, something like:

```python
solver.check() # Takes time
solver.check() # Takes same amount of time
```

However, in-between, i have not modified the solver in any way. While this seems small, in `pySym`, I may be repeatedly calling check on the same arguments many times. I can add caching on my side as well, but I think this would be beneficial for other projects too.

Also, it would be good to copy that with the `solver.translate` method.

Sorry if I'm missing it and it's already implemented somewhere!

Edit: Actually, it looks like because of how `translate` copies, even caching on my side won't fully solve the cache problem as I will have to re-model after translate.

issue_title: bug in  arm64g_calculate_condition
issue_body:
-seems to be a bug in angr/engines/vex/ccall.py relating to handling B.LT ARM64 assembly instruction

def arm64g_calculate_condition(state, cond_n_op, cc_dep1, cc_dep2, cc_dep3):
    .....
    elif concrete_cond in (ARM64CondGE, ARM64CondLT):
        nf, c1 = arm64g_calculate_flag_n(state, cc_op, cc_dep1, cc_dep2, cc_dep3)
        vf, c2 = arm64g_calculate_flag_v(state, cc_op, cc_dep1, cc_dep2, cc_dep3)
        flag = inv ^ (1 & ~(nf ^ vf))   
    ------>  # bug happens here because nf is BV64 and vf is a BV96, and claripy operation for "^" complains that lengths are not equal

The attached file:
     
![bug](https://user-images.githubusercontent.com/29239083/32283448-91bbe5e8-bee1-11e7-85d6-f9477e6d1670.png)

... is binary (named bug.png, IT IS NOT a png, but is named that so it passes the filetype filter...) with these ARM64 instructions to show the bug:
000000000000000                 CMP             W3, #0x20
0000000000000004               B.EQ            0x10
0000000000000008               B.LT            0x14

Below is a sample program demonstrating the bug.

import angr

def run_it():

    #l = logging.getLogger('angr')
    #l.setLevel(logging.INFO)

    p = angr.Project("bug.png", load_options={
        'main_opts': {
        'backend': 'blob',
            'custom_base_addr': 0x0,
            'custom_entry_point': 0x0,
            'custom_arch': 'AARCH64',
    },
    })
                
    state = p.factory.entry_state(addr=0x0)  # note: NO brackets around an option SET!!!

    state.regs.x3 = 0x0   # so the B.EQ  0x10 is not taken and get to bad instruction of B.LT  0x14

    sim = p.factory.simgr(state)

    while sim.active and not sim.unconstrained and not sim.errored: 
        if sim.active[0].addr >= 0xc:
            print("End of code reached, all done, exiting\n")
            break
        sim.step()

    print("\n\n Ended processing\n\n")
    print("sim: %s\n" % sim)

    for s in sim.errored:
        print("errored state: %s\n" % s)

if __name__ == '__main__':
    run_it()

issue_title: Build error: VexControl has no member named ‘strict_block_end’
issue_body:
There are some codes in `pyvex.c` that assign value to the `strict_block_end` field of variable `vc` of type `VexControl`:
```
pyvex.c:130:4: error: ‘VexControl {aka struct <anonymous>}’ has no member named ‘strict_block_end’
  vc.strict_block_end = 0;
        
pyvex.c:318:4: error: ‘VexControl {aka struct <anonymous>}’ has no member named ‘strict_block_end’
  vc.strict_block_end = strict_block_end;    
```
`VexControl` is defined in [libvex.h](https://github.com/angr/vex/blob/master/pub/libvex.h), there is no `strick_block_end` field.

issue_title: Ensure http headers added automatically only if explicitly requested.
issue_body:
Currently, on load the `http_headers` block is always set automatically to default http headers if parsing records. This is incorrect for non-http WARC records.

Instead, by default only add http headers for `response`, `request`, `revisit` if length > 0, otherwise set `http_headers=None`

Sometimes it is useful to auto-generated the http headers for other record types, for example, for replay. This can now be enabled with a new `ensure_http_headers=True` flag, which will auto-create http headers suitable for replay with status 200, and content type and content length set.

issue_title: Potential bug in A2C
issue_body:
In baselines/a2c/a2c.py, lines 98-100 are:
        self.obs = np.zeros((nenv, nh, nw, nc), dtype=np.uint8)
        self.nc = nc        
        obs = env.reset()

Shouldn't them be:
        self.obs = np.zeros((nenv, nh, nw, nc), dtype=np.uint8)
        self.nc = nc        
        self.obs[:] = env.reset()

as in PPO?

By the way, why are there copies of the same classes across the implementations of different methods? For example, the code above is part of the Runner class which is present in both, A2C and PPO. Is it to simplify the extraction of the code of a method from the rest of the code?

issue_title: How to use schema.execute?
issue_body:
In GraphiQL it is all so easy:

```
query PendingUsers($id: Int!) {
  pending: pendingUsers(id: $id) {
    email
  }
}
```

query variables:
```
{"id": "6"}
```

How to get the same result in Python, using schema.execute()?


```python
query = '''
  query PendingUsers($id: Int!) {
    pending: pendingUsers(id: $id) {
      email
    }
  }
'''

schema.execute(query)
# => Variable "$id" of required type "Int!" was not provided.

schema.execute(query, {"id": 6}) 
# => Variable "$id" of required type "Int!" was not provided.

schema.execute(query, {"id": "6"})
# => Variable "$id" of required type "Int!" was not provided.

schema.execute(query, {"$id": "6"})
# => Variable "$id" of required type "Int!" was not provided.

schema.execute(query, {"$id": 6})
# => Variable "$id" of required type "Int!" was not provided.

schema.execute(query, id=6)
# TypeError: graphql() got an unexpected keyword argument 'id'
```

Graphene documentation is very poor. It does not describe to much. There are almost no examples. Unit tests cover only some simple scenarios. :(

issue_title: vdirsyncer puts two PRODIDs in a singlefile and then pushes them back to server
issue_body:
I am running vdirsyncer 0.16.7 on Python 3.6.5 on Gentoo with a Radicale 2.1.10 server. On my server, there are a number of calendar events in a single calendar, created with a number of different pieces of software; hence, across the events, there are a few different `PRODID` values. When vdirsyncer synchronizes a pair one side of which is the server via `caldav` and the other side of which is a `singlefile` collection, if a new event has been added on the server, when vdirsyncer fetches the event and adds it to the `singlefile`, if the `PRODID` is different to the one present in the file, it adds it. This results in a single `.ics` file with two `PRODID` rows, which vdirsyncer then tries to push back to the server in every event; the server, in turn, rejects all the events because they have two `PRODID`s. I then have to edit the `singlefile` and delete on of the `PRODID`s (it doesn’t seem to matter which), after which vdirsyncer no longer tries to push all the events. Even if the `PRODID` in the `singlefile` is different than the `PRODID` on the server for a particular event, vdirsyncer doesn’t seem to try to replace it (which is good, since it’s obviously wrong for most of the events no matter what value it’s set to); it’s only if a second one appears in the file that vdirsyncer tries to push all the events up to the server.

I actually don’t even want vdirsyncer to push changes from the `singlefile` to the server *ever*, but, well, GH-453 isn’t done yet, so I don’t quite have that option (except for the hacky way). But some people probably do want this, so this bug would affect them.

issue_title: Don't use strictly greater than requirements
issue_body:
Now that we've broken lockstep, we need a way to specify that Certbot or a plugin relies on unreleased changes in acme or Certbot.

Suppose the latest released version is 0.25.0 and a package relies on unreleased changes in acme. To do this, we've been using version requirements like `acme>0.24.0`. This is because acme `0.25.0.dev0` satisfies this requirement and assuming we don't release `0.24.1` (in which case we'd have to change the requirement to `acme>0.24.1`), the only packages that satisfy this are `acme>=0.25.0`.

This is unconventional though, has caused confusion with multiple package maintainers, and could cause issues in the extremely unlikely event we release a bug fix release for `0.24` after `0.25.0` comes out.

@erikrose, do you have any thoughts on this? Is this something you think is worth changing and any suggestions on how to do it?

issue_title: Certbot apache module - how to have different SSL vhosts file with dummy cert
issue_body:
For some if my configurations I want the SSL/TLS Apache config files to be very different from the plain HTTP ones.

The trick at https://github.com/certbot/certbot/issues/1266#issuecomment-223744069 to specify placeholders 

```
    SSLEngine on
    SSLCertificateFile    /etc/ssl/certs/ssl-cert-snakeoil.pem
    SSLCertificateKeyFile /etc/ssl/private/ssl-cert-snakeoil.key
```

Has stopped working as you now get an error loke this:

```
# ./certbot-auto install --noninteractive --apache --domains deadlockempire.4delphi.com --cert-path /etc/letsencrypt/live/deadlockempire.4delphi.com/fullchain.pem --fullchain-path /etc/letsencrypt/live/deadlockempire.4delphi.com/fullchain.pem --key-path /etc/letsencrypt/live/deadlockempire.4delphi.com/privkey.pem
Saving debug log to /var/log/letsencrypt/letsencrypt.log
Error while running apache2ctl configtest.

AH00526: Syntax error on line 45 of /etc/apache2/vhosts.d/deadlockempire.4delphi.com-ssl.conf:
SSLCertificateFile: file '/etc/ssl/certs/ssl-cert-snakeoil.pem' does not exist or is empty

The apache plugin is not working; there may be problems with your existing configuration.
The error was: MisconfigurationError("Error while running apache2ctl configtest.\n\nAH00526: Syntax error on line 45 of /etc/apache2/vhosts.d/deadlockempire.4delphi.com-ssl.conf:\nSSLCertificateFile: file '/etc/ssl/certs/ssl-cert-snakeoil.pem' does not exist or is empty\n",)
```

This is a bit of an issue as now there seems to be a chicken-and-egg problem:

1. at the time of generating those SSL/VHOST files you do not know the exact certificate locations
2. certbot needs to have valid certificate locations in order to insert its own locations

What would be a good way to work around this?

--jeroen

issue_title: Suggest to add mesage on renew
issue_body:
At `certbot renew` when "Cert not yet due for renewal" (but --dry-run is fine), add the messagem 
> it is not recommended but you can renew with `--force-renewal`

See typical problem at https://community.letsencrypt.org/t/impossible-renew/41909

User take decision, and need a clue for your decision.

issue_title: MAVExplorer not recognizing voltage data
issue_body: MAVExplorer/MAVProxy seems to not be recognizing voltage data for newer (3.6?) ArduCopter versions. Apparently this was changed from CURR.Volt to BAT.Volt. So now on newer dataflash logs this battery related data doesn't appear to be recognized by MAVExplorer at all.

issue_title: Proxying Xpra is not working
issue_body:
I've tried to proxy [Xpra](https://www.xpra.org/) [html5 client](https://xpra.org/trac/wiki/Clients/HTML5) with `nbserverproxy` (version 0.8.3), but it doesn't seem to work.
When I start Xpra on a local machine with the
```
xpra start --bind-tcp=0.0.0.0:14500 --html=on --start=xterm
```
command and open `localhost:14500`, I see `xterm` in my browser.

I've tried to run this command from the `jupyter` terminal and then access `.../proxy/14500`.
First of all, the HTML page tries to load `js` (or `css`) from `.../proxy/js/` instead of `.../proxy/14500/js`. Ok, I've added a slash to the url and then the page was able to load.

But then, probably because it can't connect to websockets, the [`index.html`](https://github.com/totaam/xpra-html5/blob/master/index.html) page redirects me to [`connect.html`](https://github.com/totaam/xpra-html5/blob/master/connect.html) page, which locally happens only when I stop X server. There is Wiki page about proxying Xpra with Nginx: https://www.xpra.org/trac/wiki/Nginx

issue_title: Gracefully handle known transaction response
issue_body:
## Problem Definition

Under some circumstances the node may send the exact same transaction twice. When this happens ethereum node will return an error for the transaction call. (As reported by #1061)

## Solution

One possible solution is to handle the exception, search for the correct transaction hash from the transaction poll, and return that transaction hash.

issue_title: derived hosts.
issue_body:
We have had examples of 

```
hosts:
     h1:
        queue: short
        job_template: ... ${queue}
      h2:
        queue: long
        job_template: ... ${queue}
```

so it makes sense to copy definition of a host to another while allowing modification. In this way, the above could be written as

```
hosts:
     h1:
        queue: short
        job_template: ... ${queue}
      h2:
        based_on: h1
        queue: long
```

An additional use of this mechanism is to create alias for a long name. For example, if there are many hosts in `hosts.yml` that are difficult to remember, users could do

```
sos config --global --set hosts.A.based_on a_very_long_name
```
and then `A` could be used as an alias to `a_very_long_name`.

Implementation wise, it can be trouble to handle nested `based_on`, even chained `based_on`.

issue_title: Add option `-e` to action bash and run
issue_body:
`bash -e` will exit the script whenever a command yields non-zero exit code.

For example, currently

```
eh
echo 'a'
```
would produce an error message but the script would exit with code 0. The script would exit with `bash -e` as interpreter.

I am adding`-e` as default unless there are reasons why we should not do this.

issue_title: TopologyException happens while loading data for a polygon region
issue_body:
### Background

I'm using [agdc_statistics](https://github.com/GeoscienceAustralia/agdc_statistics) to process data from all of time for polygonal regions around Australia.

### Expected behaviour
The `datacube.utils.geometry.unary_union()` function is being used to `Union` the `valid_data` polygons from my source datasets (~1900), it should return a valid polygon for all of the sources.

The relevant line of code is:

`sources_union = geometry.unary_union(source.extent.to_crs(geobox.crs) for source in sources)`

### Actual behaviour
A `TopologyException` is raised instead of a valid shape being returned.

`ERROR 1: TopologyException: found non-noded intersection between LINESTRING (1.7e+06 -2.20922e+06, 1.7e+06 -2.23041e+06)
 and LINESTRING (1.7e+06 -2.3e+06, 1.7e+06 -2.2087e+06) at 1699999.9999999993 -2210399.5855833939`



### Steps to reproduce the behaviour
Perform a unary union on the valid data regions from these dataset ids: [datasetids.txt](https://github.com/opendatacube/datacube-core/files/1037347/datasetids.txt)

OR

Perform a unary union on these geometries: [source_geoms_wkt.txt](https://github.com/opendatacube/datacube-core/files/1037351/source_geoms_wkt.txt)

The exception occurs at [geometry.py#L644](https://github.com/opendatacube/datacube-core/blob/develop/datacube/utils/geometry.py#L644) which is a call to the underlying `ogr` library.

`union = geom.UnionCascaded()`


### Environment information

* Which ``datacube --version`` are you using?
    1.3.2 
* What datacube deployment/enviornment are you running against?
    Running on the NCI, loaded from `agdc-py3-prod`

issue_title: Make tests work better as examples
issue_body:
reported by @yadudoc 

The site tests require user-specific configuration ('username', 'overrides', etc). If a test isn't configured, we want pytest to skip it. The way we currently deal with this is to collect all of the user-specific options in `user_opts.py`, and if the dictionary there does not contain an entry for the site, we skip testing that site. This works well for testing (as you configure sites, more site-specific tests will be run each time you run pytest). One drawback is that it divides the relevant config information between two files, which makes it harder to pass on a configuration as an example to a user. It might be better to remove `user_opts.py` and explicitly include a `pytest.skip` in each site configuration along with dummy `FIXME` example fields. When one completes a configuration they can remove the skip option. In that way, you could point a user directly to the example configuration and say "change all of the `FIXME` fields to the appropriate value."

issue_title: Remove zone.index dependency
issue_body:
I've observed that dedupe uses zone.index for indexing; zone.index is GPL licensed and according to GPL license terms dedupe should be  GPL licensed too. And making dedupe GPL licensed creates many issues; the major one being the dedupe.io, which uses dedupe should be made open source to comply with the new terms.   

Removing the dependency on GPL licensed code can solve all the legal issues here.

issue_title: python-gitlab actually requires requests>=2.4.2
issue_body:
This code: 

```python
gl = gitlab.Gitlab(
    url=get_server_url(),
    private_token=get_private_token()
)
gl.auth()
```

...works with a recent version of requests, resulting in the client being authenticated. When `requests<=2.4.2` is installed, it results in a stacktrace ending in (this particular trace came from an environment with `requests==1.2.3`):

```
...
    gl.auth()
  File "/usr/local/lib/python2.7/site-packages/gitlab/__init__.py", line 211, in auth
    self._token_auth()
  File "/usr/local/lib/python2.7/site-packages/gitlab/__init__.py", line 233, in _token_auth
    self.user = self._objects.CurrentUserManager(self).get()
  File "/usr/local/lib/python2.7/site-packages/gitlab/exceptions.py", line 251, in wrapped_f
    return f(*args, **kwargs)
  File "/usr/local/lib/python2.7/site-packages/gitlab/mixins.py", line 67, in get
    server_data = self.gitlab.http_get(self.path, **kwargs)
  File "/usr/local/lib/python2.7/site-packages/gitlab/__init__.py", line 728, in http_get
    streamed=streamed, **kwargs)
  File "/usr/local/lib/python2.7/site-packages/gitlab/__init__.py", line 684, in http_request
    files=files, **opts)
TypeError: __init__() got an unexpected keyword argument 'json'
```

The `json` argument was added in version 2.4.2:

https://github.com/requests/requests/commit/8f17741849edb5e7eba0356f90cb17e43c938a2f

I don't know that it makes sense to lock requests to a release from 2014, but it appears that the current lock of `requests>=1.0` is insufficient.

issue_title: Customize quantity and size of percentile bins in Mapbox
issue_body: PR: https://github.com/ibm-watson-data-lab/pixiedust/pull/682 As a data scientist, I want to cluster geospatial data points into percentile groups completely customized on data quantity and size. Previously, a user wishing to display their data in a Mapbox render cannot divide data into anything other than 5 bin groups. They also are unable to determine the percentiles bounding the clusters in which the points fall. These changes add a "Number of Bins" slider to the right-side options pane to change from the default 5 to a number from 2-20 bins. ![bin_options_customization](https://user-images.githubusercontent.com/5528748/40662467-6c4ab806-6324-11e8-9609-362f6723864e.png) By default, the bins are of equal size (ex 5 bins: 0, 0.25, 0.50, 0.75, 1), but may be changed by clicking the Bin Options button to customize each percentile point on the map. Form validation ensures that each point must be between [0.0 and 1.0], and that the points are in ascending order.
